description: Yeísmo is the merger of the palatal lateral approximant /ʎ/ (as in "lluvia") into the palatal approximant /ʝ/ (as in "yema") in Spanish. This phonological change is widespread across Latin American dialects and many parts of Spain, representing a major sound shift in modern Spanish phonology.
sources: Penny, R. (2002). _A History of the Spanish Language_. Cambridge University Press.
Lipski, J. M. (2005). _Varieties of Spanish in the United States_. Georgetown University Press.
Lipski, J. M. (2013). _Latin American Spanish_. Cambridge University Press.
